Now knows ordinary lethal shots do not matter against Arthas until Jaina opens the seam, and that Frostmourne can stay dangerous even after its wielder dies.
Now knows Uther reached the aftermath in time to matter as chamber authority but not in time to save Terenas or witness the murder itself.
Now knows Frostmourne remains active in palace custody, can pull cold through foundations, and is already probing for another mind from the vault above the cells.
Learns Antonidas is physically present at the tribunal but politically muzzled, while Uther’s testimony can be cut down because he did not witness the murder itself.
Now knows the Convocation's disavowal was spoken publicly by Vereesa but not universally obeyed, because Lor'themar arrives anyway with three hundred Farstriders and two dozen mages.
Learns Jaina’s estimate has collapsed from unknown span to days, with elven mages able to feel the vault’s wrongness through the paving outside the lower court.

arcHistory.currentPosition: The lie is still active, but the chapter forces her to admit the problem is already inside her own grid and can no longer be dismissed as external noise.
The lie has broken at the operational level. Sylvanas still does not offer trust or comfort, but she is now acting on the assumption that the southern road and the elven border are one problem.
Chapter 3 turns Jaina's read into load-bearing evidence inside Sylvanas's decision process. Authority still stays in Sylvanas's hands, but the mage is part of the map now.

Chapter 4 turns that truth practical. Sylvanas still holds authority and still offers no softness, but she is now solving the plague through a fused method: Jaina reads the magic, Sylvanas reads the human and logistical system, and neither answer is complete alone.

Chapter 5 turns Jaina from necessary evidence source into live method. Sylvanas still commands the ride and still offers no softness, but now changes pace, order, and final route off Jaina's revisions quickly enough that the partnership has become operational rhythm rather than reluctant consultation.

Chapter 6 pushes Sylvanas one step further from border logic into command skepticism. She is no softer than before, but now the story proves her faster read of rank and refusal is not cynicism at all — it is survival-grade accuracy.

Chapter 7 finishes the shift from supervised reliance to autonomous split-labor trust. Sylvanas still owns movement, threat reading, and the body end of the work, but she now treats Jaina’s unseen solve as part of the same instrument rather than a risk to be watched.

Chapter 8 turns split-labor trust into survival instinct. Sylvanas no longer merely allows Jaina's unseen work to matter; now she moves on Jaina's live warnings in the middle of pursuit, proving the partnership functions fastest when neither woman pauses to translate herself.

Chapter 11 kills the last border fiction. Sylvanas successfully activates human force, immediately sees the catastrophe vector inside that success, and turns north because the missing mage has become the most urgent hole in the entire campaign.

Chapter 11 kills the last border fiction. Sylvanas successfully activates human force, immediately sees the catastrophe vector inside that success, and turns north because the missing mage has become the most urgent hole in the campaign.

Chapter 12 keeps Sylvanas out of sight until the last line and still proves how load-bearing she has become. Jaina recognizes her first as missing friction returned: the human cost, the exit-read, the tactical question arriving too late to stop the theorem but in time to break the room open.
Chapter 13 lets Sylvanas win the room and lose the ease beneath the partnership. She gets Jaina out, kills Kel'Thuzad, and keeps the road moving, but trust has shifted from reflex into confirmation.

Chapter 13 converts that return into scar. Sylvanas remains the body-cost check and the woman who gets everyone out alive, but now she has seen enough externals to begin treating Jaina's reliability as something that must be confirmed rather than simply used.

Chapter 14 refuses repair without allowing withdrawal. Sylvanas keeps Jaina under pressure, rejects institutional drag, and chooses to keep riding with the compromised source because the source is still the best chance the road has. Trust remains checked, but the partnership remains in motion.

Chapter 15 keeps Sylvanas’s mistrust intact but corrects one part of her sanctum read. She still sees Arthas as a fuse and Jaina as a damaged source, yet she now has visible proof that Jaina is trying to spend the answer for rescue, not for self-justification.

Chapter 16 gives Sylvanas external proof strong enough to survive mistrust. She witnesses Jaina use forbidden knowledge for immediate rescue, watches the method work, and then watches timing break it anyway. The chapter moves her from guarded recommitment into hard witness at the edge of Arthas's next irreversible choice.

Chapter 17 destroys Sylvanas’s last hope that Arthas might remain a hard ally inside a bounded answer. She ends the chapter as witness rather than partner, holding two truths at once: the Culling works tactically, and that fact damns the man who chose it.

Chapter 18 turns witness into anchor. Sylvanas does not repair the chapter's damage with speeches; she moves Jaina, rebuilds formation, and quietly accepts that north has become the real question even while the road still runs south.
Chapter 20 proves care can ride entirely through authority. Sylvanas remains external-only, but the voyage makes her the line that decides when Jaina stops, turning command itself into trust.

Chapter 20 carries that broken border lie onto open water. Sylvanas no longer only orders the road north; she becomes the competence Jaina trusts enough to stop for, which makes the partnership's care visible without ever turning soft.

Chapter 21 proves superior execution cannot recover time once the enemy has already changed category. Sylvanas still trusts command and skill, but now she aims that trust past Arthas the prince and toward Frostmourne the actual field problem.

Chapter 22 moves Sylvanas from co-investigator and protector into explicit sacrificial timing partner. The plan stops belonging to an abstract formation and lands in her own body the instant she says I.

Chapter 23 moves Sylvanas from named timing partner to the woman who spends her own body to make the spell real, ends Arthas herself, and then has to stand inside the political wreckage of having done it.

arc.lie: Clear borders and obvious enemies are the threats a commander can truly solve first.
arc.truth: The real answer may lie in who can still alter the road before force reaches the wall, even if that answer comes from compromised knowledge and not from command rank.
arc.currentPosition: Chapter 24 forces Sylvanas into the form of helplessness she hates most: perfect comprehension without freedom of action. The only arc movement available is that outside reinforcement finally reaches her after the verdict, turning total abandonment into a narrow but real next move.
